method_atom: the study of a lattice of coupled binary variables subject to quenched disorder — either randomness in the pairwise coupling strengths or random local biasing fields — and the characterization of its equilibrium critical behavior and phase transitions as a symmetry-breaking control parameter and the disorder strength are varied, including the regime of strong random local fields at high occupation density.
object_atom: dilute anisotropic antiferromagnetic materials in two and three dimensions, realized experimentally with varying magnetic-ion concentration under an applied magnetic field.
brief_reason: the quenched-disorder lattice model of binary variables with random couplings/fields and its critical behavior is the transferable method, while the dilute anisotropic antiferromagnet is the condensed-matter-specific object it is realized in.
